title: Kung Pao Chicken

overview.description: A classic Sichuan stir-fry combining tender chicken, roasted peanuts, dried chillies and a perfectly balanced sweet, sour and savoury sauce. Bold, aromatic and surprisingly quick to cook.

method: Mix the cornflour with the water to make a smooth slurry.
Combine the light soy sauce, dark soy sauce, black vinegar, Shaoxing wine, sugar and chicken stock in a small bowl.
Heat a wok over a high heat until smoking hot.
Add the vegetable oil followed immediately by the dried chillies and Sichuan peppercorns. Stir-fry for about 20 seconds until fragrant, taking care not to burn them.
Add the chicken and stir-fry for 5–6 minutes until lightly browned.
Add the garlic, ginger and spring onions and cook for another 30 seconds.
Pour in the prepared sauce and bring to a simmer.
Stir in the cornflour slurry until the sauce becomes glossy and lightly coats the chicken.
Fold through the roasted peanuts just before serving.

learning.did-you-know: Kung Pao Chicken is named after Ding Baozhen, a 19th-century governor of Sichuan Province. 'Gong Bao' was one of his official titles.
learning.what-you-will-learn: How Sichuan peppercorns create their famous tingling sensation, how to toast aromatics without burning them and how to balance sweet, sour and savoury flavours.

notes.why-its-in-the-chapman-kitchen: A true Sichuan classic that introduces completely different flavours from the Cantonese dishes already in the planner. It also makes excellent use of pantry ingredients once you've stocked them.
chapman-tips: Don't skip the Sichuan peppercorns—they're what makes the dish unique.
Roasted unsalted peanuts give the best flavour.
Have every ingredient prepared before heating the wok.
notes.batch-cooking: Can be frozen, although the peanuts lose some crunch. For the best texture, add freshly roasted peanuts when reheating.
notes.leftovers: Reheat quickly over a high heat and serve with freshly cooked rice.
